mnemonics.title: McCall Model Categories
mnemonics.content: Remember the three McCall categories using: O-R-T (Operation, Revision, Transition) - Think 'Open-Repair-Transfer' for each product stage.

mnemonics.title: ISO 9126 Six Characteristics
mnemonics.content: Use acronym FRUEPM: Functionality, Reliability, Usability, Efficiency, Portability, Maintainability - 'Fast Reliable Users Enjoy Porting Modernly'

mnemonics.title: QA vs QC
mnemonics.content: QA = Prevention (Process), QC = Detection (Product) - Remember: 'A' for Avoid (prevent), 'C' for Catch (detect)

mnemonics.title: ISO 25010 Eight Characteristics
mnemonics.content: FPCURMS: Functional suitability, Performance efficiency, Compatibility, Usability, Reliability, Security, Maintainability, Portability

acronyms.usage: Product-focused activities to detect and fix defects
11 factors in 3 categories: Operation, Revision, Transition
Sets international standards for software quality

acronyms.expansion: Functionality, Reliability, Usability, Efficiency, Portability, Maintainability
Functional suitability, Performance efficiency, Compatibility, Usability, Reliability, Security, Maintainability, Portability

visualTips.visualization: Draw a 3-level pyramid - Top: Product Operation (Correctness, Reliability, Efficiency, Integrity, Usability), Middle: Product Revision (Maintainability, Flexibility, Testability), Bottom: Product Transition (Portability, Reusability, Interoperability)
Create a Venn diagram showing ISO 9126 (6 circles) inside ISO 25010 (8 circles) with Security added as new outer circle
Tree diagram with Quality at root, characteristics as main branches, sub-characteristics as smaller branches, and metrics as leaves

analogies.analogy: Like the quality of a car - safety (reliability), fuel efficiency (efficiency), ease of driving (usability), easy to repair (maintainability), works on different fuels (portability)
QA is like preventive healthcare (checkups, healthy habits), QC is like diagnostic testing when you're sick - one prevents, one detects
Quality models are like fitness assessments - they measure different aspects (strength, flexibility, endurance) to give a complete picture of health
